name: Prompt Starter Library Builder
description: Turn rough tasks or prompt ideas into a reusable prompt starter library.

assignments.name: library_goal
assignments.value: Create a clean prompt library for reusable AI workflows, content production, SEO planning, research, and productivity systems.

assignments.name: target_user
assignments.value: Creators, consultants, students, and solo builders who want reusable prompts instead of losing useful prompts in chat history.

assignments.name: tasks_or_prompt_ideas
assignments.value:
Task 1: Turn messy notes into tasks.
Task 2: Rewrite this article intro in a clearer, less salesy tone.
Task 3: Create five newsletter subject lines for an AI workflow update.
Task 4: Summarize these research notes and suggest three practical next steps.
Task 5: Review this n8n workflow idea and point out where it may break.

assignments.name: preferred_categories
assignments.value: Content, SEO, Productivity, Workflow Design, Research, Review

assignments.name: tag_style
assignments.value: Use short lowercase tags with hyphens, like content-writing, workflow-design, research-summary, human-review.

assignments.name: output_style
assignments.value: Markdown prompt library with an index, one reusable entry per prompt, copy-ready prompt text, variables, use cases, and quality notes.

parameters.jsCode:
const input = { ...$json };

const prompt = [
  'You are an AI prompt architect.',
  '',
  'Your job is to turn rough user tasks into reusable, copy-ready starter prompts.',
  '',
  'Important behavior rules:',
  '- Do not write Python.',
  '- Do not write JavaScript.',
  '- Do not explain how to process the data.',
  '- Do not create a script, function, pseudo-code, or algorithm.',
  '- Do not echo the input data back to the user.',
  '- Do not include Markdown outside JSON.',
  '- Return one JSON object only.',
  '',
  'Workflow: GP-WF-004 - Prompt Starter Library Builder',
  'Problem it solves: People know what they want AI to help with, but they do not always know how to write a reusable prompt.',
  'Main task: Convert rough tasks, workflow ideas, or prompt fragments into a structured prompt library.',
  '',
  'Input data:',
  JSON.stringify(input, null, 2),
  '',
  'For each task in tasks_or_prompt_ideas:',
  '- Generate one reusable starter prompt.',
  '- Preserve the task intent.',
  '- Improve it into a complete prompt someone can copy and use.',
  '- Include role, context, task, output format, constraints, and human review guidance inside copy_ready_prompt.',
  '- Use placeholders such as {notes}, {draft}, {audience}, {goal}, {workflow_description}, {research_notes}, {constraints}, or {source_material}.',
  '- Make copy_ready_prompt useful and specific, usually 90 to 180 words.',
  '- Keep the tone practical, clear, and non-salesy.',
  '',
  'Return ONLY valid JSON in this exact shape:',
  '{',
  '  "document_title": "Prompt library title",',
  '  "summary": "Plain-English summary of what this library helps with.",',
  '  "library_name": "Library name",',
  '  "library_goal": "What this library helps the user do.",',
  '  "categories": ["Category 1", "Category 2"],',
  '  "prompt_entries": [',
  '    {',
  '      "title": "Short prompt title",',
  '      "category": "Best-fit category",',
  '      "description": "What this prompt helps with",',
  '      "use_case": "When someone should use it",',
  '      "tags": ["tag-one", "tag-two"],',
  '      "variables": ["{variable_name}"],',
  '      "copy_ready_prompt": "Full starter prompt the user can copy and run",',
  '      "quality_note": "What to check before trusting the output"',
  '    }',
  '  ],',
  '  "organization_notes": ["How to maintain or expand this prompt library"],',
  '  "missing_context_questions": ["Question that would make future prompts better"],',
  '  "next_steps": ["Practical next step 1", "Practical next step 2", "Practical next step 3"],',
  '  "review_note": "Short human review reminder."',
  '}',
  '',
  'Final reminder: output JSON only. No code. No prose before the JSON. No Markdown fence.'
].join('\n');

return [{ json: { ...input, prompt } }];
